summary: Builds a small attachment-name parameter object from a supplied path-like string and an optional plain-text fallback policy.
signature_intent: Accepts a required string-like name input and an optional fallback control, returning undefined for an omitted name or an object of header-style name parameters.
behavior: Returns undefined when the primary input is omitted.
Requires the primary input to be a string.
Defaults the fallback control to enabled when it is omitted.
Requires the fallback control to be either a boolean or a string.
Rejects a fallback string that cannot be represented as ISO-8859-1.
Uses only the final path segment of the supplied name.
Adds an extended name field when a plain quoted form is insufficient, when escaping is needed, or when a distinct fallback exists.
Adds a plain name field when the final name is safe for quoted use or when a distinct fallback exists.
When fallback is enabled as a boolean, derives a Latin-1-compatible fallback from the final name when possible.
When fallback is provided as a string, uses its final path segment as the plain fallback.
edge_cases: A disabled boolean fallback prevents generation of a derived fallback.
A fallback equal to the final primary name is treated as no distinct fallback.
An unsafe final name may produce only the extended name field when no distinct fallback is available.
A safe final name with no distinct fallback may produce only the plain name field.
A fallback string may include path separators; only its final segment is used.
forbidden_details: No variable names, repository names, source paths, or unnecessary implementation quirks are included.
